name: Hlin
description: Hlin is a Norse goddess whose name means "protector" or "coverer". She is one of Frigg's handmaidens and is tasked with protecting those Frigg wishes to shield from danger. Hlin listens to the prayers and pleas of mortals and reports them back to Frigg, who then decides whether or not to intervene. She is a goddess associated with comfort, protection, and consolation in times of need. While not featured prominently in many surviving myths, her role as a protector and a close confidante of Frigg underscores her importance within the Norse pantheon. Some interpretations suggest she is an aspect of Frigg herself, representing the goddess's protective and nurturing qualities. Hlin's power resides in her ability to hear and relay the concerns of mortals to Frigg, acting as an intermediary between the human realm and the divine. She embodies the concept of divine intervention and the hope that the gods are listening to the suffering of humanity. Her association with Frigg, the goddess of marriage, motherhood, and home, further emphasizes her role as a protector of the family and the vulnerable. Ultimately, Hlin represents the compassionate and protective side of the divine, offering solace and hope to those in need. She is a symbol of the unseen forces that work to safeguard individuals and communities from harm. Her existence assures mortals that their cries for help do not go unheard, and that the gods are capable of extending their protection to those who seek it.
subtitle: Protector and Consoler of Mortals

keyMyths.title: Hlin's Protection
keyMyths.description: Hlin listens to the prayers of a mother whose child is in danger and relays the message to Frigg, who then intervenes to save the child. This showcases her role as a protector.

keyMyths.title: Secrecy and Knowledge
keyMyths.description: Hlin is entrusted with secrets by Frigg, demonstrating her loyalty and importance within Frigg's inner circle. She is a trusted confidante.

keyMyths.title: Frigg's Companion
keyMyths.description: Hlin is mentioned as being in the company of Frigg, solidifying her position as a handmaiden and close associate of the powerful goddess. This illustrates her close relationship with the queen of the Aesir.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
